rulings.Case Title: CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC. VS. A&B PAINTING, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re A&B Painting , Inc.'S Duty To Defend
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re A&B Painting , Inc.'S Duty To Defend The motion is denied without prejudice. The Court determines that the trial judge should rule on the duty to defend issue. Denying the motion will avoid the practical difficulties of sorting out multiple, and potentially conflicting, duties to assume the active defense of litigation in progress. See Crawford v. Weather Shield Mfg. Inc. (2008) 44 Cal.4th 541, 565, fn.12. Plaintiff is not prejudiced because it admits that it is currently receiving a defense. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/ehg

rulings.Case Title: DAVID LAI VS. RICHARD J. STRATTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition To Confirm Arb Award
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANT RICHARD STRATTON, HANSON BRIDGETT, LLP's Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award Granted. The petition to confirm arbitration award is not procedurally defective. Plaintiff cites to no legal authority in support of its argument that Defendants are required to file a cross-complaint on a matter that has been arbitrated. " ' "[A] point which is merely suggested by [a party's] counsel, with no supporting argument or authority, is deemed to be without foundation and requires no discussion." [Citation.]' [Citation.]" (Do It Urself Moving & Storage, Inc. v. Brown, Leifer, Slatkin & Berns (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 27, 35, superseded by statute on other grounds in Union Bank v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 573, 582.) The use of Judicial Council forms is optional and their use is not a bar to recovery. (Vapnek, Cal. Practice Guide: Prof'l Responsibility (Rutter) 5:1143a.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/ehg

rulings.Case Title: DIGITAL REALTY TRUST, LP, A MARYLAND LIMITED et al VS. CAPSTAR COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE SERVICES, LTD., A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Severing And/Or Bifurcating Into Separate Trials
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANTs CAPSTAR COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE SERVICES, LTD., A, CCRES, LTD., A TEXAS LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, CASSIDY TURLEY, INC., A MISSOURI CORPORATION and CASSIDY TURLEY COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE SERVICES, Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Severing And/Or Bifurcating Into Separate Trials The motion to bifurcate is denied. Bifurcation will not serve the ends of justice because Mr. Bunnett and Mr. Patterson, who are located in Texas, are key witnesses as to the underlying liability and successor liability issues. The possibility that bifurcation will lead to them testifying twice weighs against granting the instant motion. rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEVENS VS. JOHN MILLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Vacate Discovery Orders And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2014, Line 8, CROSS-COMPLAINANTs TOURAJ BOUSSINA and PERRY BOUSSINA's Motion To Vacate Discovery Orders And Sanctions Denied. The discovery orders entered on July 9, 2013 and on September 5, 2013 from which Defendants and Cross-Complainants Touraj and Perry Bousssina seek relief were valid. The Boussina's attorney did not contest the motions or appear at the haring, and pursuant to Local Rule 10.B.1, the Boussinas are deemed to have stipulated to the motions being decided by the temporary judge who was appointed by the Court. The instant motion is distinguishable from Rooney v. Vermont Investment Corp. (1973) 10 Cal.3d 351, 357-358, where the defendants were not notified of any hearing date. Here, the Boussinas were notified of the hearing dates and failed to contest the tentative rulings or appear at the hearings. The Boussinas have not demonstrated good cause under CCP § 473(b) for relief from the July and September orders. The declarations of the Boussinas themselves, and the Declaration of their new counsel James Dombroski also do not fulfill the requirements of Section 473(b) for mandatory relief from the orders since they are not a declaration by the attorney who committed the mistake "attesting to his or her mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or neglect." The declaration of former counsel Jonathan Fried does not demonstrate good cause for discretionary relief from the discovery orders as all discovery responses at issue in the prior orders were due before the accident on August 30, 2013. Mr. Fried does not establish excusable neglect on his part in failing to oppose the motions heard on July 9, 2013 and September 5, 2013 given that the Boussina's oppositions to both motions were due before accident date of August 30, 2013 upon which Mr. Fried based his notice of unavailability. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: URS CORPORATION DBA URS CORPORATION AMERICAS, A VS. UNIVERSAL MOLDING EXTRUSION COMPANY, INC. DBA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To 1) Compel Further Deposition Of Dominick Baione, 2) Tro Compel Dominick Baione To Answer Depsotiin Question, 3) For Issue/And Or Evidentiary Sanctions And 4) For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2014, line 1 - PLAINTIFF URS CORPORATION DBA URS CORPORATION AMERICAS' Motion To 1) Compel Further Deposition Of Dominick Baione, 2) Tro Compel Dominick Baione To Answer Depsotiin Question, 3) For Issue/And Or Evidentiary Sanctions And 4) For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN BRUCE VS. YOUNG K. CHO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Contempt And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2014, Line 9, PLAINTIFF STEVEN BRUCE's motion For Contempt And Sanctions Granted, as to Form Interrogatory no. 14.2 and Request for Production (RFP) no. 10. Denied as to RFP no. 7. For Form Interrogatory no. 14.2, Defendants have agreed to amend the response. For RFP no. 7, no further response is required, Defendants aver in their verified response that all documents have been produced. For RFP no. 10, Defendants shall amend their response and state whether the document produced was redacted by Defendants in any way or whether the document was received by Defendants by a third party in its current redacted form. rulings.Case Title: RT WESTERN MAINTENANCE INC. VS. JOHN BERTOLDI INCORPORATED, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Deft Jbi'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2014, line 2 - PLAINTIFF RT WESTERN MAINTENANCE INC.'s Motion To Compel Defendant Jbi'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Set One. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant John Bertoldi Inc. has not satisfied its duty to meet and confer in a reasonable and good faith manner. Parties are ordered to meet and confer in advance of the hearing on January 16 in an effort to reach an informal resolution of the issues in this motion. If the parties are able to reach a resolution in advance of the hearing, counsel for moving party RT Western Maintenance shall notify the court. If the parties are not able to reach a resolution in advance of the hearing, counsel shall appear and be prepared to discuss the status of meet-and-confer discussions as well as the merits of this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RT WESTERN MAINTENANCE INC. VS. JOHN BERTOLDI INCORPORATED, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Deft John Bertoldi'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2014, line 2 - PLAINTIFF RT WESTERN MAINTENANCE INC.'s Motion To Compel Defendant John Bertoldi'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Set One. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant John Bertoldi has not satisfied his duty to meet and confer in a reasonable and good faith manner. Parties are ordered to meet and confer in advance of the hearing on January 16 in an effort to reach an informal resolution of the issues in this motion. If the parties are able to reach a resolution in advance of the hearing, counsel for moving party RT Western Maintenance shall notify the court. If the parties are not able to reach a resolution in advance of the hearing, counsel shall appear and be prepared to discuss the status of meet-and-confer discussions as well as the merits of this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RT WESTERN MAINTENANCE INC. VS. JOHN BERTOLDI INCORPORATED, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Fmtn To Comple Deft Jbi'S Further Responses To Req.'S For Production-Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2014, line 2 - PLAINTIFF RT WESTERN MAINTENANCE INC.'s Motion To Comple Defendant Jbi'S Further Responses To Req.'S For Production-Set One. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant John Bertoldi Inc. has not satisfied its duty to meet and confer in a reasonable and good faith manner. Parties are ordered to meet and confer in advance of the hearing on January 16 in an effort to reach an informal resolution of the issues in this motion. If the parties are able to reach a resolution in advance of the hearing, counsel for moving party RT Western Maintenance shall notify the court. If the parties are not able to reach a resolution in advance of the hearing, counsel shall appear and be prepared to discuss the status of meet-and-confer discussions as well as the merits of this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. rulings.Case Title: MARK BELANGER VS. WINE.COM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT WINE.COM DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant's demurrer is overruled as to the second and third causes of action in Plaintiff's Amended Complaint ("AC."). Considering the Foley v. Interactive Data Corp. (1988) 47 Cal.3d 654, 680-681, factors, Plaintiff alleges: he worked for Defendant from the fall of 2004 through July 2012 (AC 4, 11); Defendant encouraged Plaintiff to keep his CPA status active (AC 21); and Defendant covered Plaintiff's license fees and continuing education costs. (Id.) Reading the AC in a light most favorable to the Plaintiff, these facts are sufficient to show Defendant's conduct overcomes the at-will presumption. (See Labor Code § 2922.) Because Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts regarding the existence of an implied-in-fact contract, Defendant's demurrer to Plaintiff's claim for breach of the covenant of good faith and fair dealing is overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/ehg

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK A. MISSUD VS. STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANT STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Granted. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Defendant has carried its burden of showing the alleged conduct in Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint arises from protected activity under CCP § 425.16(e). Plaintiff has not demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the merits because he fails to produce prima facie evidence supporting his defamation claim. (CCP § 425.15(b)(1).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: PERSOLVE, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, DBA, VS. RAFAEL S LEIVA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2014, Line 16, DEFENDANT RAFAEL LEIVA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Overruled. (CCP § 430.10(e), (f) & (g).) Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege the first - third causes of action. (CCP § 430.10(e).) The pleadings are not uncertain. (CCP § 430.10(f).) The Complaint is based on common counts, and a contract. The requirements of CCP § 430.10(g) is inapplicable to common counts causes of action.
